summary: Retested Nexus public ingress deployment against the Cloudflare main URL. Backend ingress endpoints and S3 status passed; home and chat UI loaded with polished gate/shell. Local type-check and production build also passed.

integration_issues.issue: Browser console logs show 404 prefetch attempts for /call and /channels base routes (dynamic routes exist as /call/[peerId] and /channels/[id]).

action_items: Fix/disable homepage prefetch links targeting non-existent /call and /channels index routes to remove 404 console noise.
Re-run browser console verification after prefetch route cleanup.
MOCKED: Keep /api/media/s3/status behavior until real AWS credentials are provided (currently configured=false with missing keys).
MOCKED: Public exposure is still via Cloudflare quick tunnel, not a permanent owned domain/DNS setup.
critical_code_review_comments: apps/api-gateway/src/routes.ts is 1400+ lines (>700 guideline) and should be split into focused route modules.
S3 status endpoint correctly avoids secret values; only missing variable names are exposed.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Main ingress URL now reachable and validated from this environment. Pytest suite updated to assert ingress home/chat/health/ready/s3 status with 5/5 pass. Browser automation confirms polished login gate on /chat.
rca of the issue: Console 404s are caused by requests to /call and /channels without required dynamic params. Route manifest includes /call/[peerId] and /channels/[id], so prefetching base paths is invalid. Mitigation: update links/prefetch targets to valid concrete routes or disable prefetch on placeholder links.
